key_insight: A physics-informed machine learning model trained on sparse experimental data identifies alloying rules that can improve room-temperature ductility in tungsten-based refractory high-entropy alloys.
engineering_relevance: The results offer an interpretable screening framework to prioritize W-based fusion alloys with better ductility, especially by favoring moderate Ti, Ni, and Co additions and avoiding high Cr content.
method: Curated experimental dataset of tungsten-containing alloys
Compared three classifiers with nested cross-validation; SVC performed best
Used SHAP to rank key physics-based descriptors and mapped synthetic compositions on pseudo-ternary diagrams
evidence_signal: The SVC generalized best under nested cross-validation, and its predictions matched known experimental trends such as Ti-assisted ductility and Cr-related embrittlement.
watch_item: The dataset is sparse and heterogeneous, so predictions should be treated as guidance for targeted validation rather than direct design rules.
paper_title: Ductility Design Rules for Tungsten based Refractory High Entropy Alloys from Sparse Experimental Datasets
